content: NATURAL SELECTION CONCEPT: Run multiple bots with different strategies simultaneously. Eliminate losers, iterate winners. Over thousands of iterations, best strategies emerge organically — like biological evolution. KEY INSIGHT: First batch of AI-generated strategies was universally terrible — ALL 5 lost money. Bots struggle to create profitable strategies from scratch without GUIDANCE. Knowledge (transcripts) provides the foundation.

COMPETITION FORMAT: 5 bots per race, $1,000 starting capital each, 3 hours per race. Three risk tiers: Moderate → Higher → Maximum. Bottom performers eliminated, replaced in next round. Platform: Hyperliquid (on-chain derivatives). 15 total bots across 3 races. Results: only 4 out of 15 made money. Aggregate: $15,000 in → $15,074 out = $74 profit. Pareto distribution: most P&L from few heavy hitters.

RACE 1 — MODERATE RISK (ALL LOST): Bot 1: Funding Harvester (exploit funding rate differentials). Bot 2: Rubber Band (mean reversion from extremes). Bot 3: Basis Bot (cash-and-carry arbitrage). Bot 4: Fade Machine (counter-trend fading). Bot 5: Old Guard (classic trend following). ALL FIVE unprofitable. Basis Bot was worst — $1,000 wallet devastated. Lesson: conservative strategies with no edge = guaranteed losses.

RACE 2 — HIGHER RISK (FIRST PROFITABLE): Bot 6: Breakout Trader (momentum breakout plays) — made $78 on WIF meme coin, long at $0.233, exit at $0.252 with leverage. Bot 7: Old Diverge (divergence-based entries) — extreme volatility, fell off cliff then recovered then crashed. Bot 8: Liquidation Hunt (trade liquidation clusters). Bot 9: Pairs Trader (correlated asset spread) — also profitable. Bot 10: Sentiment Trader (news-driven). First profitable batch — 2 out of 5 made money.

RACE 3 — MAXIMUM RISK (BIGGEST WINNER): Bot 11: Scalper (high-frequency) — burned through capital in fees. Bot 12: YOLO Bot (maximum leverage, all-in) — made 40x LEVERAGE LONG on Bitcoin with $1,030, soared to $175.20 profit = WINNER. Bot 13: Contrarian (counter-trend). Bot 14: Whale Follower (copy-trade profitable wallets) — barely traded, spent time looking for wallets. Bot 15: Meme Coin Sniper (target meme breakouts). Profitable batch thanks to YOLO Bot.

STRATEGY INPUT APPROACHES: 1) RESEARCH — have AI research most profitable strategies autonomously. 2) COPY TRADING — download profitable trader's brain (trades, commentary, stops, reasoning) and feed to bot. 3) LEADERBOARD MINING — pull strategies of Hyperliquid's most profitable traders (making tens of millions). 4) NATURAL SELECTION — run competition, eliminate losers. BEST APPROACH: combine curated knowledge with natural selection. Knowledge = foundation, competition = refinement.

WHAT WORKED vs WHAT DIDN'T: WORKED: Aggressive strategies with conviction (YOLO Bot 40x long, Breakout Trader momentum plays, Pairs Trader spread). DIDN'T WORK: Conservative strategies with no edge (Funding Harvest, Basis Arb, Fade Machine, Old Guard trend following). LESSON: In competition format, aggressive strategies with good risk management beat conservative strategies. The conservative bots bled slowly, the aggressive ones either won big or lost big.

EVOLUTION METHODOLOGY: Take winning bot configurations and use as starting points for new bots. Provide more sophisticated strategy prompts based on what worked. Scale up: more bots, more races, more iterations. Combine winners: merge best elements of multiple winning strategies. Run hundreds of competitions = evolution of trading bots over time. The potential is infinitely powerful — best strategies emerge organically through competition and elimination.

RISK TIER ANALYSIS: Moderate risk (Race 1) — conservative position sizing, tight stops. All 5 bots lost = too conservative, no edge. Higher risk (Race 1) — larger positions, wider stops. 2 of 5 profitable = better. Maximum risk (Race 3) — maximum leverage, aggressive sizing. 1 massive winner offset losses = best results. LESSON: Risk must be calibrated to edge quality. No edge + low risk = slow death. Good edge + high risk = high returns (if managed).

OPENCLAW SYSTEM: OpenClaw is an AI agent that controls your entire computer through terminal. People run it on Mac Minis, server racks, virtual servers. For the first time, AI can autonomously execute tasks including trading crypto. Setup: compute hardware → LLM agent (Claude recommended) → trading platform (Hyperliquid) → strategy inputs. The agent analyzes market data, makes decisions, and executes trades — all autonomously.
